title: Review kopia index-blob trend; decide on repo/bucket split
description:
Follow-up to the volsync kopia cache-limit work (commits 232de13a / 2b2e9ed1).

## Context
All ~25 apps back up into ONE shared kopia repository: bucket "volsync-kopia" on
nas0.internal:3900 (Garage S3). The maintenance mover logs "Found too many index
blobs (N)" on every repo open. On 2026-05-30 the count was trending UP, not flat:
  00:30Z ~3730 -> 08:30Z ~4479 -> 16:30Z ~5206  (~+750 every 8h, ~+40%/day)
Full maintenance runs each 8h cycle yet the count still rises, so compaction is
not keeping pace with backup write rate. Only 3 same-day samples were visible at
the time (CronJob history limit), so the weekly shape was unknown.

## What was already changed
kubernetes/apps/volsync-system/volsync/maintenance/kopiamaintenance.yaml:
set spec.successfulJobsHistoryLimit: 30 (3 runs/day x 10 days) so the trend is
observable. This issue is deferred ~10 days to let that history accumulate.

## HOW to check the trend
export KUBECONFIG=/home/pjl/ws/home-ops/kubeconfig
for p in $(sak k8s get pod -n volsync-system --path ".metadata.name" | tr -d "\"" | awk "/maint/"); do
  start=$(sak k8s get pod "$p" -n volsync-system --path ".status.startTime" | tr -d "\"")
  cnts=$(sak k8s logs "$p" -n volsync-system </dev/null | sak fs grep "too many index blobs" - | awk -F"[()]" "{print \$2}" | sort -un | tr "\n" " ")
  printf "%s  blobs: %s\n" "$start" "$cnts"
done | sort
# Each row = one maintenance run; "blobs" is the index-blob count(s) seen that run.
# Also worth a look in a full-maintenance log: the "GC found N unused contents
# too recent to delete" line shows how much is held back by kopia safety margins.

## Decision criteria
- Count PLATEAUS over the 10 days -> benign warning, no action; close.
- Count keeps CLIMBING -> tune maintenance first (dedup-preserving), in order:
    1. kopia safety margin (the "too recent to delete" retention) so GC/compaction
       can actually drop+compact more per full-maintenance pass.
    2. epoch / index-compaction settings so full maintenance collapses the index.
    3. reduce snapshot churn: trim hourly retention (currently 24 hourly + 7 daily
       per app x ~25 apps) on low-value apps.
- Only consider splitting into multiple repositories (per-app or per-tier) if you
  specifically want BLAST-RADIUS ISOLATION. Splitting does NOT fix the write-vs-
  compaction imbalance and it COSTS cross-app dedup; it just keeps each repo under
  the warning threshold longer. Note: index-blob count is per-REPOSITORY, not per
  BUCKET -- multiple buckets only matter for separate S3 creds/quotas/ACLs.
notes:
## Early check 2026-06-04 (5 days of ~30-run history, gaps on 05-31/06-05)
Reading as PLATEAU / benign, NOT the climbing case. Original 05-30 alarm
(3730->4479->5206 same day) was the rising edge of one sawtooth tooth mistaken
for a monotone trend. With multi-day data it's a steady sawtooth: each full
maintenance compacts the index back down, backups push it up before the next.

Post-compaction FLOOR (per-day low): 06-01 2992 -> 06-02 2991 -> 06-03 2958 ->
06-04 2883. Flat, trending slightly DOWN -> compaction is keeping pace.
Pre-compaction PEAK (per-day high): ~5213 -> 5225 -> 5938 -> 5110. Flat ~5.1-5.9k;
one bump to 5938 on 06-03 08:30 within noise.

Per decision criteria this is the "count plateaus -> benign, close" branch: no
maintenance tuning, no repo/bucket split needed on current evidence. Let it ride
to the 06-09 deferral to confirm the floor stays flat through weekend backup load.

title: volsync: pin kopia cache hard limits to stop cache PVC overflow
description:
ROOT CAUSE
volsync-src-zwave-js-ui backup job failed: kopia could not write /cache/kopia.config
(ENOSPC) — the 6Gi kopia cache PVC was full (~379k files). Ceph itself was healthy
(11TiB free), so it was the dedicated cache PVC, not the backing pool.

The perfectra1n/volsync operator (internal/controller/mover/kopia/mover.go
calculateCacheLimits) auto-allocates kopia cache hard limits as metadata=70% +
content=20% = 90% of cacheCapacity, "leaving 10% for other files". On a small cache
PVC that 10% sliver cannot absorb kopia's /cache/logs, kopia.config, temp files, and
between-sweep overshoot (kopia limits are soft, swept every few minutes; see
kopia/kopia#4140), so the PVC fills and the backup fails. The mover image's entry.sh
header comment claims an auto-calc default but does NOT implement it (only applies a
limit when KOPIA_*_CACHE_SIZE_LIMIT_MB env is set); the real auto-calc lives in the
operator at the too-aggressive 90%. Percentages are hardcoded constants, not
configurable.

FIX (done in working tree, not yet committed)
Pin kopia cache hard limits explicitly to content=20% / metadata=40% of cacheCapacity
(60% total, ~40% headroom) via the CRD fields contentCacheSizeLimitMB /
metadataCacheSizeLimitMB.
- components/volsync/replicationsource.yaml + replicationdestination.yaml: new
  substitution vars VOLSYNC_KOPIA_CONTENT_CACHE_MB:=1024 / VOLSYNC_KOPIA_METADATA_CACHE_MB:=2048
  (sized for the 5Gi default).
- 25 app ks.yaml files: proportional per-app overrides next to VOLSYNC_CACHE_CAPACITY
  (Flux postBuild can't do arithmetic, so values are precomputed). The four apps at
  exactly 5Gi inherit the component default.

FOLLOW-UPS
- Recover zwave-js-ui: cache PVC was deleted and ReplicationSource paused during triage
  — unpause (spec.paused=false) and confirm a clean backup once this is reconciled.
- Commit + Flux reconcile; verify a backup on a small-cache app (zwave/zigbee2mqtt) and
  a large one (plex) under the new limits.
- Consider upstream report to perfectra1n/volsync: operator 90% allocation has too
  little headroom for small caches, and the entry.sh "auto-calc" comment is stale/
  misleading (not implemented in the script).
close_reason: Fixed by pinning kopia cache hard limits (content=20%/metadata=40% of cacheCapacity, ~40% headroom) via contentCacheSizeLimitMB/metadataCacheSizeLimitMB, replacing the operator's too-tight 90% auto-calc. Shipped in two commits: canary (volsync component + zwave-js-ui + plex) then rollout to the remaining 23 apps. zwave-js-ui cache PVC was recreated and is backing up Successfully.

title: Fix volsync component sourceNamespace null rendering (latent CRD-validation breakage)
description:
The reusable volsync component renders an invalid ReplicationDestination on any fresh apply, and will break every existing volsync-backed app the moment its ReplicationDestination is re-applied (ks change, CRD bump, or forced reconcile).

## Root cause
`kubernetes/components/volsync/replicationdestination.yaml:31` has:

    sourceIdentity:
      sourceName: ${APP}
      sourceNamespace: ${VOLSYNC_SOURCE_NAMESPACE:=}

No app sets `VOLSYNC_SOURCE_NAMESPACE`, so it expands to empty, which YAML renders as `null`. The current volsync CRD rejects this:

    ReplicationDestination "<app>-dst" is invalid: spec.kopia.sourceIdentity.sourceNamespace: Invalid value: "null": spec.kopia.sourceIdentity.sourceNamespace in body must be of type string

Existing apps (e.g. convertx, applied 2026-01-18) have stored objects whose sourceIdentity contains only `sourceName` — the empty value was pruned under the older CRD — so they currently pass. They are NOT safe: a re-apply re-runs the dry-run and will fail the same way. tracefinity (the first new volsync app since the CRD tightened) hit this and is blocked.

## Recommended fix
Per the CRD schema, `sourceNamespace` is OPTIONAL: "If not specified, defaults to the namespace of this ReplicationDestination." Only `sourceName` is required. Every app here has its ReplicationSource and ReplicationDestination in the same namespace, so the default is always correct.

Drop the line entirely from the component:

    sourceIdentity:
      sourceName: ${APP}

This fixes all apps at once and matches the behavior already stored for existing objects. Then force-reconcile each volsync app (or let the next reconcile re-apply) to confirm the dry-run passes.

## Stopgap already in place
`kubernetes/apps/self-hosted/tracefinity/ks.yaml` sets `VOLSYNC_SOURCE_NAMESPACE: self-hosted` as a per-app workaround. Remove that override once the component is fixed.

## Affected apps (29, all use components/volsync)
database/pgadmin; download/qbittorrent, sabnzbd; game/romm; home/esphome, home-assistant, zigbee2mqtt, zwave-js-ui; llm/ollama, open-webui; media/calibre-web-automated, lidarr, pinchflat, plex, radarr, sonarr, tautulli, tdarr; observability/gatus; self-hosted/convertx, filabridge, jrommanager, manyfold, paperless, spoolman, syncthing, tandoor, wordpress-gds, wordpress-poc.

## Verification
After the component fix + reconcile, for each app:
  kubectl get replicationdestination <app>-dst -n <ns> -o jsonpath="{.status.conditions}"
and confirm no ReconciliationFailed events on the owning Kustomization.
close_reason: Fixed in component: removed the empty sourceNamespace line from kubernetes/components/volsync/replicationdestination.yaml (sourceNamespace is optional and defaults to the RD's own namespace, which is always correct here). Also removed the per-app VOLSYNC_SOURCE_NAMESPACE stopgap from tracefinity's ks.yaml. Fixes all 29 volsync apps at once. After deploy, force-reconcile a couple of existing apps to confirm dry-run passes.

title: Address tdarr-node OOM issues
close_reason: Increased tdarr-node memory request from 256Mi to 512Mi and limit from 1Gi to 4Gi. The 1Gi limit was too low for GPU-accelerated video transcoding workloads.

title: Add Envoy Gateway outlier detection (BackendTrafficPolicy) to eject dead upstream endpoints
description:
On 2026-05-20, auth.chezlawrence.com (and any app fronted by Envoy Gateway) is vulnerable to a hard outage when an upstream's pod IPs change but the running envoy proxies hold a stale EDS snapshot. Root cause of that incident: after the envoy-gateway v1.7.3 -> v1.8.0 upgrade (commits 65fa5c6 + a070962), the envoy-external proxies last fully synced 05-17 ~20:35; authentik-server pods rolled 05-18 13:43 and got new IPs; the EDS delta never reached the running proxies, so Envoy kept dialing 3 dead IPs (10.192.0.214 / 10.192.2.204 / 10.192.3.27) with hundreds of cx_connect_fail each. Restarting envoy-gateway (control plane) did NOT help; only restarting envoy-external (data plane) cleared it.

What turned a transient xDS glitch into a 100% outage: the route's cluster has NO active health check and NO outlier (passive) detection, so Envoy left the dead endpoints flagged health_flags::healthy and kept load-balancing onto them indefinitely. Outlier detection would have ejected the failing endpoints after N consecutive connect failures and let the request retry a (nonexistent-but-soon-correct) healthy host / fail fast instead of hanging 10s per request.
design:
Add an Envoy Gateway BackendTrafficPolicy enabling passive outlier detection (and optionally connection retry budget). Decide scope:
- Cluster-wide: target the envoy-external (and envoy-internal) Gateway via targetRefs (kind: Gateway) so every backend inherits ejection. Simplest, broad protection.
- Per-route: target individual HTTPRoutes (kind: HTTPRoute) for fine control.

Recommended starting point (gateway-scoped) — tune thresholds:
  spec.outlierDetection:
    consecutive5XxErrors / consecutiveLocalOriginFailures (connect failures): ~5
    interval: 10s
    baseEjectionTime: 30s
    maxEjectionPercent: 100 (so a fully-stale cluster can eject all and fail fast rather than hang)

Place under kubernetes/apps/network/envoy-gateway/ (e.g. a new gateway/ or app/ manifest) and wire into the kustomization. Verify the v1.8.0 CRD (BackendTrafficPolicy, gateway.envoyproxy.io/v1alpha1) supports the chosen fields. Note: no existing BackendTrafficPolicy in the repo yet.
acceptance_criteria:
1. A BackendTrafficPolicy with outlier detection is applied and Accepted (status conditions) on the envoy-external gateway (and envoy-internal if in scope).
2. Simulated dead endpoint (e.g. scale a backend's pods so IPs change, or blackhole one) is ejected from the cluster within baseEjectionTime instead of receiving 100% of traffic — confirm via envoy admin /clusters (health_flags shows ejection) and absence of sustained cx_connect_fail growth.
3. Document in the envoy-gateway README the operational note: roll envoy-external/envoy-internal proxy deployments after any envoy-gateway version upgrade to avoid stuck EDS.
